name: RBC Heritage
summaryBlurb: Harbour Town is a precision-first week that rewards approach control, fairways, wedge proximity, and repeat course comfort more than raw power.
courseTraits: Approach play is still the main engine of the model.
Driving accuracy and bogey avoidance matter more than distance.
Course history has more signal here than most PGA TOUR stops.

name: Wells Fargo Championship
summaryBlurb: Quail Hollow leans toward complete tee-to-green players, with long-iron quality, difficult par-4 scoring, and bogey avoidance driving the safest profiles.
courseTraits: Approach and par-4 scoring create real separation here.
Complete ball-striking matters more than short-term putting spikes.
High-floor Top 40 builds often work better than pure volatility.
